name: finishing-branches
description: Use when work is complete and ready for integration, merge, or PR creation.
capabilities: Run pre-merge checks including tests and linting
Perform security scans for leaked secrets
Review changes with git diff commands
Create pull requests using automated tools
Apply PR templates for consistent documentation
author: BPSAI
securitySummary: All 26 static findings are false positives. This is a pure markdown documentation file containing git workflow guidance. The 'bash' code blocks are documentation examples showing standard git commands, not executable shell code. No cryptographic algorithms, C2 infrastructure, or actual external commands exist in this file. The skill is safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: This skill provides a complete workflow for finishing feature branches. It guides you through pre-merge checks, security scans, code review, and automated PR creation.
userTitle: Finish branches for merge and PR creation
limitations: Does not execute commands automatically - user must run them
Requires bpsai-pair CLI tools to be installed for some commands
Cannot create PRs without GitHub remote configured

useCases.title: Complete feature work
useCases.description: Finish feature branches with proper checks before creating pull requests

useCases.title: Enforce merge standards
useCases.description: Ensure all branches meet security and quality standards before integration

useCases.title: Streamline contributions
useCases.description: Apply consistent PR templates and checklists across all contributions

promptTemplates.title: Complete current branch
promptTemplates.prompt: Finish this branch. Run all checks, scan for secrets, review changes, and create a PR.

promptTemplates.title: Quick merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Quick finish this branch. Run the quick finish commands and push to remote.

promptTemplates.title: Security check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run security scans on my staged changes. Check for any leaked secrets or credentials.

promptTemplates.title: Create PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request using the PR template. Include summary, changes, and testing notes.

bestPractices: Always run security scans before committing sensitive changes
Use PR templates to ensure consistent documentation
Clean up local branches after successful merges
antiPatterns: Skipping pre-merge checks to save time
Creating PRs without testing locally first
Leaving debug statements or TODOs in committed code
Merging without reviewing the diff stat first

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: Requires bpsai-pair CLI tools installed. Git and GitHub integration for PR creation.

faq.question: Can I use this without bpsai-pair?
faq.answer: Yes, the commands shown are standard git patterns. The bpsai-pair commands are optional conveniences.

faq.question: Does this create PRs on all platforms?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on GitHub. Other platforms may need different commands or integrations.

faq.question: Is my code safe when running security scans?
faq.answer: Yes, secret scans run locally on your machine. No credentials or code are sent to external services.

faq.question: What if tests fail during checks?
faq.answer: The skill will report failures. Fix the issues before proceeding with merge. Tests must pass.

faq.question: How is this different from other PR tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides a complete workflow including pre-merge validation, not just PR creation.
